rationale: clauses: produces a manifest under release/(KILLED: redirect > release/device-history-record.json -> /tmp/… — step lookup and run_text assertion both fail), records tag(KILLED: tag removed wholesale from jq --arg + object), commit SHA(KILLED: commit_sha removed wholesale), releasing actor(KILLED: released_by removed wholesale), timestamp(KILLED: released_at removed wholesale), artifact list(KILLED: artifacts removed wholesale), attached to the release alongside the copies(KILLED: files: release/* narrowed to release/*.pdf; the manifest-outside-release/ probe also kills this path). The "each document release" trigger clause is covered by the DI-3 test (doc-* tag trigger). SURVIVED (near-equivalent, documented): dropping only the --arg commit_sha line passes because $commit_sha remains in the jq object — that mutant makes the workflow error at run time rather than emit a wrong record. NOT COVERED: field bindings — mutating --arg commit_sha "${{ github.sha }}" to a constant string SURVIVED; the assertions pin jq field/variable names, not the ${{ github.* }}/date bindings, so a manifest recording wrong tag/SHA/actor/timestamp values would still pass.
uncovered_clauses: manifest fields record the actual release context: binding commit_sha (and by symmetry tag/actor/timestamp/artifacts) to a constant survives — the test asserts field names, not the ${{ github.* }} bindings
